text: There's a new proposal backed by Google and Microsoft that could be shaping the future of how we use the web. And I kind of like it. It's called WebMCP, but don't get that confused for a normal MCP server. Instead, WebMCP is actually a browser API, and it will let front-end developers expose features of their sites as tools to AI agents, essentially letting every site become a mini MCP server. And while you may have already seen some sites start off their own MCP servers already, this is a little bit different. Its goal is to actually help agents use the website for you instead of just accessing your APIs and showing that in a chat. It'll be entirely front-end based. title: NEW Browser API That Makes AI Agents Actually Work (WebMCP)
